segments.text: Some time later Jesus left to go to the other side of the Sea of Galilee (also known as the Sea of Tiberias).
A large crowd was following him, for they'd seen his miracles of healing.
Jesus went up a hill and sat down there with his disciples.
The time for the Jewish festival of the Passover was approaching.
When Jesus looked up, and saw a large crowd coming towards him, he asked Philip, {
Where can we buy bread to feed these people?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He only asked this to see how Philip would respond, because Jesus already knew what he was going to do.
Two hundred silver coins[^39] wouldn't buy enough bread to give everyone only a little,
segments.speaker: Philip

segments.text: Philip replied.
One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, spoke up.
There's a boy here who has five barley loaves and a couple of fish, but what good is that when there are so many people?
segments.speaker: Philip

segments.text: Have everybody sit down,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus said. There was plenty of grass there, so they all sat down, the men numbering around five thousand.
Jesus took the bread, gave thanks, and had it handed out to the people as they sat there. Then he did the same with the fishes, making sure the people had as much as they wanted.
Once they were all full, he said to his disciples, {
Collect what's left over so nothing is wasted.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So they collected and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves the people had eaten.
When the people saw this miracle, they said, {
Surely this is the Prophet who was to come into the world.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus realized that they were about to force him to become their king, so he left them and went up into the hills to be by himself.
When evening came, his disciples went down to the sea,
climbed into a boat, and headed across the water towards Capernaum. By now it was night and Jesus had not joined them yet.
A strong wind began blowing and the sea grew rough.
When they had rowed three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the sea, coming towards the boat. They were very frightened.
Don't be afraid!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: he told them.
It's me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then they gladly took him into the boat, and immediately they reached the shore where they were going.

The next day the crowd that had stayed on the other side of the sea noticed that there had been only one boat there, and that Jesus had not got into the boat with his disciples, but they had left without him.
Then other boats arrived from Tiberias, landing near to the place where they'd eaten the bread after the Lord had blessed it.
Once the crowd realized that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and went over to Capernaum, looking for Jesus.
When they found him on the other side of the sea, they asked him, {
Rabbi, when did you get here?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I tell you the truth,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied,
you're looking for me because you ate as much bread as you wanted, not because you understood the miracles.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Don't be preoccupied about food that doesn't last, but concentrate on the lasting food of eternal life which the Son of man will give you, for God the Father has placed his seal of approval on him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So they asked him, {
What should we be doing in order to do what God wants?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied, {
What God wants you to do is to trust in the one he sent.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What miracle are you going to perform for us to see so we can trust you? What are you able to do?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they asked.
Our forefathers ate manna in the desert in fulfillment of the Scripture that says, ‘He gave them bread from heaven to eat.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I tell you the truth, it wasn't Moses who gave you bread from heaven,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied.
It's my Father who gives you the true bread of heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For the bread of God is the one who comes from heaven and gives life to the world.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Lord, please give us this kind of bread all the time!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they said.
I am the bread of life,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied.
Anyone who comes to me will never be hungry again, and anyone who trusts in me will never be thirsty again.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But as I explained to you before, you have seen me, [^42] but you still don't trust me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: All those the Father gives me will come to me, and I won't reject any of them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For I came down from heaven not to do what I want, but to do what the one who sent me wants.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What he wants is for me not to lose anyone he has given to me, but for me to raise them up at the last day. [^43]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: What my Father wants is for everyone who sees the Son and trusts in him to have eternal life, and for me to raise them up at the last day.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then the Jews began to grumble about him because he had said, {
I am the bread that came down from heaven.
segments.speaker: The Jews

segments.text: They said, {
Isn't this Jesus, the son of Joseph? We know his father and his mother. So how can he now tell us, ‘I came down from heaven’?
segments.speaker: The Jews

segments.text: Stop grumbling to each other,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus said.
No one can come to me unless the Father who sent me attracts them, and I will raise them up at the last day.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As is written in Scripture by the prophets, ‘Everyone will be taught by God.’[^44] Everyone who listens to and learns from the Father comes to me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Not that anyone has seen God, except he who is from God; he has seen the Father.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you the truth: anyone who trusts in him has eternal life.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I am the bread of life.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Your forefathers ate manna in the desert but they still died.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But this is the bread that comes down from heaven, and anyone who eats it won't ever die.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I am the life-giving bread from heaven, and anyone who eats this bread will live forever. The bread is my flesh that I give so that the world may live.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then the Jews argued heatedly among themselves. {
How can this man give us his flesh to eat?
segments.speaker: The Jews

segments.text: they asked.
Jesus told them, {
I tell you the truth, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of man and drink his blood, you cannot truly live.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Those who eat my flesh and drink my blood have eternal life, and I will raise them up at the last day.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For my flesh is true food, and my blood is true drink.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Those who eat my flesh and drink my blood remain in me, and I remain in them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Just as the life-giving Father sent me and I live because of the Father, so anyone who feeds on me will live because of me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Now this is the bread that came down from heaven, not the kind your forefathers ate and still died. Anyone who eats this bread will live forever.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus explained this while he was teaching in a synagogue at Capernaum.
Many of his disciples when they heard it said, {
This is hard to accept! Who can follow[^45] it?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus saw that his disciples were complaining about this, so he asked them, {
Are you offended by this?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then what if you were to see the Son ascend to where he was before?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The Spirit gives life; the physical body doesn't do anything. [^46] The words I've told you are spirit and life!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Yet there are some of you who don't trust me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: (Jesus had known from the very beginning who didn't trust him, and who would betray him.)
Jesus added, {
This is why I told you that no one can come to me unless it is made possible[^47] by the Father.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: From this time on many of Jesus' disciples gave up and no longer followed him.
Then Jesus asked the twelve disciples, {
What about you? You don't want to leave as well, do you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Simon Peter answered, {
Lord, who would we follow? You're the one who has the words of eternal life.
segments.speaker: Simon Peter

segments.text: We trust in you, and we're convinced that you are God's Holy One.
segments.speaker: Simon Peter

segments.text: Jesus replied, {
Didn't I choose you, the twelve disciples? Yet one of you is a devil.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: (Jesus was referring to Judas, son of Simon Iscariot. He was the one of the twelve disciples who would betray Jesus.)
